title: Fix expiration bypass: enforce real-time expiration checks on downloads and block expired attachments on frontend

requirements.summary: Perform fresh expiration check before file/image downloads to prevent downloading expired pastes
acceptanceCriteria: If a paste expires while the page is open and the user clicks download, the download is blocked and the expired error state is shown.
The expiration check at download time queries the backend fresh, not from stale in-memory or cached state.
If the paste is still valid at download time, the download proceeds normally without disruption.

file_operations.description: Add real-time expiration validation before file downloads. When the user clicks a download button, re-fetch the paste from the backend to verify it hasn't expired. If the fresh backend check indicates the paste is expired, abort the download and transition to the expired error state. If valid, proceed with the download. This ensures that even if the paste expires while the page is open, downloads are blocked based on current backend state rather than stale cached data.

Add a lightweight query hook for real-time expiration validation that bypasses TanStack Query cache. This hook should accept a pasteId and password, call the backend's getPaste or getRemainingTime method with cache disabled, and return whether the paste is currently expired. This enables PasteViewPage to perform fresh backend checks at download time without relying on potentially stale query cache.
